title: Teramnus hedysaroides
intro: Teramnus hedysaroides, also known as the horse gram or Indian hog plum, is a leguminous plant native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Teramnus hedysaroides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make capsules, tablets, and ointments for its therapeutic uses.
taxonomy: Teramnus hedysaroides is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical Africa. This plant is a perennial climber that belongs to the genus Teramnus, which is part of the subfamily Faboideae. The classification of Teramnus hedysaroides is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Fabales, Family: Fabaceae, Genus: Teramnus, Species: T. hedysaroides. Within the genus Teramnus, Teramnus hedysaroides is often placed in the tribe Phaseoleae.
